title: Real Estate Deals in Bismarck ND: A Complete Investor's Guide for 2024
excerpt: Bismarck, North Dakota is quietly becoming one of the Midwest's most attractive markets for real estate investors. Discover the best neighborhoods, deal types, and strategies to find profitable real estate deals in Bismarck ND.
author: AIWholesail Team
tags: real estate deals in Bismarck ND
Bismarck North Dakota real estate investing
metaDescription: Find profitable real estate deals in Bismarck ND. Explore top neighborhoods, average prices, wholesale, flip & rental strategies, and tips for investors.
metaKeywords: real estate deals in Bismarck ND, Bismarck North Dakota real estate investing, wholesale real estate Bismarck, investment properties Bismarck ND, Bismarck ND rental properties, flip houses Bismarck, off-market deals Bismarck ND

sections.content: When most investors think of hot real estate markets, cities like Phoenix or Nashville come to mind. But real estate deals in Bismarck ND are quietly delivering strong returns for investors who know where to look. As the state capital of North Dakota, Bismarck offers a stable economy, low unemployment, and affordable home prices that are hard to find in larger metros. Whether you are wholesaling your first deal or building a rental portfolio, this guide breaks down everything you need to know about investing in Bismarck. If you are brand new to the game, start with our [Real Estate Investing for Beginners](https://aiwholesail.com/guides/real-estate-investing-for-beginners) guide before diving in.
Bismarck ND Market Overview: Why Investors Are Paying Attention

Bismarck sits along the Missouri River and serves as the political and economic hub of North Dakota. The city has a population of roughly 75,000 and has seen steady growth over the past decade, driven largely by state government employment, healthcare, and the energy sector. Unlike oil-dependent cities in western North Dakota, Bismarck's economy is diversified enough to weather commodity price swings. That stability translates directly into real estate. Vacancy rates remain low, typically hovering between 3% and 5%, and home values have appreciated steadily without the speculative bubbles seen in coastal markets. For investors, that means more predictable returns and less volatility.
Average Home Prices in Bismarck ND

As of 2024, the median home price in Bismarck sits around $320,000, up from roughly $265,000 just five years ago. That represents healthy appreciation without pricing out investors. Entry-level single-family homes in older neighborhoods can still be found in the $150,000 to $220,000 range, which is where most wholesale and fix-and-flip opportunities exist. Mid-range homes run between $220,000 and $380,000, while newer construction in the south side of the city regularly exceeds $400,000. Average rent for a 3-bedroom home runs approximately $1,400 to $1,700 per month, giving buy-and-hold investors solid cash flow potential on lower-priced acquisitions.
Best Neighborhoods for Real Estate Investors in Bismarck
North Bismarck: Affordable Entry Points

North Bismarck offers some of the most affordable housing stock in the city. Homes here were built primarily in the 1950s through 1970s, meaning investors can find properties that need cosmetic updates rather than full gut renovations. Average purchase prices in the $140,000 to $190,000 range, combined with rent potential of $1,200 to $1,400 per month, make this a go-to area for rental property investors and wholesalers looking for motivated sellers.
South Bismarck: Strong Appreciation and Flip Potential

South Bismarck is the city's growth corridor. New subdivisions, retail development, and proximity to top-rated schools drive demand from owner-occupant buyers. This makes it an attractive target for fix-and-flip investors. After-repair values (ARVs) in south Bismarck frequently reach $380,000 to $480,000, giving flippers meaningful spread on the right deals. Use the free [ARV Calculator at aiwholesail.com/tools/arv-calculator](https://aiwholesail.com/tools/arv-calculator) to quickly estimate your upside before making an offer.
Legacy and Hillcrest Areas: Steady Rental Demand

The Legacy and Hillcrest neighborhoods attract working families and government employees who prefer to rent rather than buy. Turnover is low, tenants tend to stay 2 to 4 years on average, and vacancy is rarely an issue. For buy-and-hold investors, these established neighborhoods offer the consistency that makes rental income predictable.
Types of Real Estate Deals Available in Bismarck ND

items: Wholesale Deals: Distressed properties, probate sales, and pre-foreclosures in north Bismarck are prime wholesale targets. Typical assignment fees range from $8,000 to $20,000 depending on the deal. New to wholesaling? Read our [Wholesaling Real Estate for Beginners](https://aiwholesail.com/guides/wholesaling-real-estate-for-beginners) guide to learn the basics.

Fix and Flip: South Bismarck and newer suburban corridors offer strong ARVs. Budget approximately $25 to $45 per square foot for renovation in North Dakota, where labor and material costs remain below national averages.

Buy and Hold Rentals: Single-family rentals in the $150,000 to $220,000 price range with rents of $1,200 to $1,500 monthly can produce gross yields of 7% to 10%. Duplexes and small multifamily properties amplify those returns further.

BRRRR Strategy: Buy distressed properties in north Bismarck, renovate, rent, then refinance to pull equity and repeat. The BRRRR model works well here because purchase prices are low enough to leave room after rehab.
Tax Lien and Tax Deed Purchases: North Dakota holds annual tax deed sales where investors can acquire properties at steep discounts. Check the Burleigh County website for upcoming sale schedules.

sections.content: Pro Tip: Before committing to any deal in Bismarck, run your numbers through the free [Wholesale Deal Calculator at aiwholesail.com/tools/wholesale-deal-calculator](https://aiwholesail.com/tools/wholesale-deal-calculator). Plug in your purchase price, estimated repair costs, and ARV to instantly see your maximum allowable offer (MAO) and projected profit. Skipping this step is the number one mistake new investors make.
Local Market Trends Shaping Bismarck Real Estate in 2024

Several trends are reshaping how investors approach deals in Bismarck right now. First, inventory remains tight. The city consistently has fewer than 400 active listings, meaning competition for listed properties is real. That pressure pushes serious investors toward off-market strategies. Second, interest rates have reduced buyer purchasing power, which means more potential homeowners are renting longer — great news for landlords. Third, the Bismarck-Mandan metro area continues to attract remote workers from higher cost-of-living states who are priced out of their home markets. This in-migration is adding demand pressure on both the rental and owner-occupant sides of the market.

"The best real estate deals aren't found on the MLS — they're found by building relationships before a property ever hits the market." — Common wisdom among top wholesalers and investors in secondary markets like Bismarck.

That quote rings especially true in a smaller market like Bismarck. With a limited number of transactions happening each year, the investors who consistently win deals are those who have established pipelines of off-market leads. Learn how to build yours in our detailed breakdown of [How to Find Off-Market Real Estate Deals](https://aiwholesail.com/guides/off-market-real-estate-deals).
Practical Tips for Out-of-State Investors Targeting Bismarck

Bismarck is a compelling market for out-of-state investors, but long-distance investing requires extra preparation. The good news is that the market is small enough that a focused effort can yield real results quickly. Here are the most important steps to take before you commit capital from across the country.

items: Build a local team first: Find a real estate-savvy attorney, a property manager who specializes in single-family rentals, and a licensed contractor before you close your first deal. Bismarck is a relationship-driven market and local knowledge is invaluable.

Understand North Dakota landlord-tenant law: North Dakota is considered a landlord-friendly state, but eviction timelines and security deposit rules differ from other states. Know the rules before you rent.

Account for harsh winters in your renovation budgets: Bismarck winters are severe. Factor in costs for proper insulation, heating system inspections, and weatherproofing. Deferred maintenance on these items can wipe out your profit margin.

Use virtual tours and remote inspections strategically: Platforms that offer 3D virtual tours can help you evaluate properties without flying in, but always arrange an in-person inspection from a trusted local inspector before closing.
Study micro-neighborhood dynamics: A two-block difference in location can mean a $40,000 swing in ARV in Bismarck. Do not rely solely on broad zip code data — get granular.

Leverage technology to find deals at scale: Platforms like [AIWholesail's Bismarck ND market dashboard](https://aiwholesail.com/markets/bismarck-nd) aggregate distressed property data, equity-rich homeowners, and pre-foreclosure leads so you can identify opportunities before other investors do.

Analyze every deal before you move: Read our guide on [How to Analyze Real Estate Deals Like a Pro](https://aiwholesail.com/guides/how-to-analyze-real-estate-deals) to sharpen your underwriting skills and avoid costly mistakes.

sections.content: Pro Tip: When evaluating rentals in Bismarck, use the 1% rule as a quick screen — monthly rent should be at least 1% of your all-in purchase price. A $180,000 property should ideally rent for $1,800 per month. North Bismarck properties frequently meet or beat this threshold, making them worth a closer look.
Is Bismarck ND Right for Your Investment Strategy?

Bismarck is not a get-rich-quick market. It rewards investors who are disciplined, patient, and focused on fundamentals. If you are looking for massive appreciation plays or high-volume wholesale markets, larger cities may suit you better. But if you want steady cash flow, affordable entry prices, a diversified local economy, and a landlord-friendly legal environment, real estate deals in Bismarck ND deserve a serious look. The investors who have quietly built wealth here over the past decade did so by being consistent, doing their homework, and acting quickly when the right deal appeared.
